t_ch: 原本应该是没有的，投标完就可以等定标结果了。
t_en: No. Usually you just submit your bid and wait for the result.

t_ch: 但驭空大人希望我们能够好好辩论一番，这样才有机会从对方观点中补足自己。
t_en: But Madam Yukong wants us to have a proper debate to better understand each other's points.

t_ch: 接下来应该会有一场恶战。
t_en: She's not making it easy for us.

t_ch: 小少爷脑子灵活、目标明确、再加上资金充沛，我们确实不占优势。
t_en: Indeed. Xiaohan is eloquent and knows exactly what he wants, not to mention the funding he gets from the IPC. It will be an uphill battle.

t_ch: 但是我们要借这个机会把我们的态度展现出来。
t_en: That said, this is also our chance to make a strong case for ourselves.

t_ch: 小少爷，就从你开始吧，让我们听听你对金人巷的规划与考虑。
t_en: Xiaohan, whenever you are ready. Tell us about your plan for Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 小秘书，你们有什么要问的吗？
t_en: Mingxi, please ready your questions.

t_ch: 金人巷商会的各位好啊。
t_en: Members of Aurum Alley Merchant Guild, I hope you're all keeping well.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 万一我们不好呢？
t_en: And if we're not?

t_ch: 你确定你要从一句话就开始反驳我吗？
t_en: No need to be quite so aggressive...

t_ch: 商会利用码头物流，不仅还上了租金，还筹集到了竞标资金，这足以看出码头的重要性。
t_en: By utilizing the dock, the guild managed to repay outstanding rents and also raised sufficient funding to stand at the bidding. That just goes to show how vital the dock is.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 我们付出了很多其他的努力！
t_en: We did much more than simply utilize the dock!

t_ch: 但是你没有否定码头的重要性，无论是合作，还是物流规划，最终都服务于码头。
t_en: But you don't deny that the dock was vital to your operations, correct? Your partnership with the shop owners and the increase in cargo handling capacity — none of it would have been possible without the dock.

t_ch: 你错了，我们最终服务的是金人巷商铺。
t_en: Wrong. None of it would have been possible without winning the hearts of local businesses.

t_ch: 素裳姑娘讲得好。
t_en: Well said, Sushang.

t_ch: 哈哈，在谈判桌上就别玩文字游戏了吧。
t_en: Haha, I think you understand my point.

t_ch: 公司愿意更进一步，充分开发码头的物流效应。将金人巷打造成银河闻名的商业港口。
t_en: The IPC seeks to unleash the full potential of the dock, taking full advantage of its role as a logistics center — thus transforming Aurum Alley into one of the most advanced commercial ports in the galaxy.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 金人巷真的能够停下星际货船？
t_en: How are interastral cargo ships supposed to dock? There isn't enough room.

t_ch: 为了建设商船港，这里大部分商店需要搬出金人巷。
t_en: To address this, most of the shops will need to be relocated.

t_ch: 我们也会重新规划整个商区的布局，保证港口足够开阔，吞吐量绝不输给流云渡。
t_en: The IPC will redesign the layout of the whole area to make sure ships can move in and out smoothly. We expect at least the same trading volume as Cloudford.

t_ch: 请放心，搬迁的商铺都会拿到合适的赔偿，数字之大，足以让所有人脸上挂着笑容。
t_en: Rest assured, all the shops that need relocating will receive generous compensation from the IPC. The package we've prepared will make everyone happy.

t_ch: 公司又开始施展钞能力了……
t_en: Classic IPC...

t_ch: 这难道不是大家所期望的、终极的「金人巷复兴」吗？
t_en: And that, honorable ladies and gentlemen, is the rejuvenation that everyone has been hoping for.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 你怎么知道这就是大家期望的？
t_en: How can you be sure that everyone's vision aligns with yours?

t_ch: 难道各位高呼「金人巷复兴」只是自我满足吗？我不相信。
t_en: ...I was using a figure of speech — but our vision of "rejuvenation" will benefit the majority.

t_ch: 不对，<color=#dbc291ff>难道不应该问问金人巷商铺们的意见吗？</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Shouldn't you gather people's opinions first?</color>

t_ch: （我们可以用什么证明？）
t_en: (Do we have any proof?)

t_ch: 出示
t_en: (Present evidence)

t_ch: 金人巷的商铺们可不这么想！
t_en: The shops think otherwise!

t_ch: 这是从金人巷各商户处收集来的意向调查，其中大部分人与商会建立了合作，并且希望能够保留现在的经营模式——其中包括同功坊。
t_en: According to the poll we conducted earlier with business owners, most of them have already partnered with the Merchant Guild and wish to preserve their current business model. Artistry Hardware is among the shops that registered this answer.

t_ch: 家父只是个固执迂腐的匠人，不必以他的意见为参考。
t_en: ...Father has never been the shrewdest when it comes to business matters. I'm afraid his opinions don't constitute the most valuable insights.

t_ch: 就算有很多金人巷商户们期望如此，但这并不代表他们有远见卓识。
t_en: In agreeing with your approach, those shops may risk missing the bigger picture... and opportunities for future growth.

t_ch: 那不是远见，那是目中无人。
t_en: So you think they don't know what's best for themselves.

t_ch: 小少爷，你把自己看得太高了。
t_en: You think too highly of yourself.

t_ch: 明曦，接下来请讲讲商会对金人巷的规划与考虑。
t_en: Mingxi, please share with us what the guild has in mind.

t_ch: 小少爷将码头看作的优势，但是对商会来说，商铺才是最大的卖点。
t_en: As Xiaohan mentioned, the IPC sees the dock as the most valuable asset the Alley has to offer, but for us, the local shops are what makes the Alley special.

t_ch: 商会不能脱离商铺存在，商会会把金人巷打造成「新•罗浮第一夜市」。
t_en: The guild serves the sole purpose of helping local businesses. We will help the Alley reclaim the title of "the best night market on the Luofu".

t_ch: 「罗浮第一夜市」？在公司注资后，仙舟罗浮上处处都会建起新的商业中心，人们为什么会专程来到你的金人巷呢？他们早就不在意谁才是「第一夜市」了。
t_en: "Best night market on the Luofu"? With the IPC's funding pouring in, hundreds of commercial centers will sprout. Why would people come to Aurum Alley when they have so many other options? Nobody cares about that title anymore.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 不是没有人会在意，我们就很在意。
t_en: Say what you want, but we care.

t_ch: 你们为什么会认为只有继续做「美食街」才算是金人巷复兴？
t_en: What makes you so determined to hold onto this idea of a "food street"? Why can't the revival of the Alley take on other forms?

t_ch: 因为人们需要金人巷的美食街，<color=#dbc291ff>这一点，我们可以证明</color>。
t_en: Because people want it to retain its food street. <color=#dbc291ff>We have proof of that</color>.

t_ch: （我们可以用什么证明？）
t_en: (Do we have any proof?)

t_ch: 出示
t_en: (Present evidence)

t_ch: 人们会始终需要金人巷美食街。
t_en: When people think of Aurum Alley, they think about its food. The two notions can't be separated.

t_ch: 这是金人巷商会和尚滋味一起做的联动活动。
t_en: We organized a crossover between the Merchant Guild and Spices Supreme.

t_ch: 借助宣传礼品、虚拟购物券等等手段，前往金人巷消费的游客比以往增加了一倍还多。你看，美食街在大家的心中仍然有重要的地位。
t_en: By sending out coupons and gifts, we attracted twice as many customers as we normally would. This shows that food is still a big reason why people visit the Alley.

t_ch: 这比重新打造优势更简单直接。
t_en: Building on an existing strength is much easier than starting out from scratch.

t_ch: 「美食街」金人巷竞争力十足。
t_en: The Alley's identity as a "food street" is what makes it so competitive.

t_ch: 小少爷，你会投入天价的资金去建设港口，你会赶走大部分的本地商铺，去换来你承诺的「复兴」，但那时金人巷还是金人巷吗？
t_en: The revival you envision involves throwing pots of money into building a port while forcing away most local businesses. Wouldn't that erase the very identity of the Alley?

t_ch: 我们想要选择「最金人巷」的未来。
t_en: Our approach protects the people who made the Alley what it is today.

t_ch: 金人巷需要在历史的演进中抓住机遇，转变自己的职责。「金人巷」在公司选择的未来里，会成为最有分量的名字。
t_en: Aurum Alley needs to adapt and reimagine itself. The future that the IPC offers will ensure its name is known in the most remote corners of the galaxy.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 名字有分量有什么用？
t_en: You are sacrificing its identity in exchange for a fancy brand.

t_ch: 我的理想是将金人巷打造成「金字招牌」，一个所有人都叫得出来的名称。
t_en: A brand so fancy that everyone will remember it.

t_ch: 「金人巷」刻在了每个人的心中，这不比默默无闻在两三百年之后消失更好？
t_en: Wouldn't it be better for the Alley to take on a new life and stay known forever? If it remains a market, I can assure you it will die quietly over the course of the next two centuries.

t_ch: 不好！
t_en: You don't know that!

t_ch: 我只想为当下的金人巷考虑。
t_en: I'm only concerned with what's best for the Alley here and now.

t_ch: 自由讨论的时间结束。请双方回去之后静候评审团消息，辛苦了。
t_en: That concludes our discussion. We will inform all participants of the result when a decision has been made. Thank you all for coming.

t_ch: 商业区开发竞标并不会当场宣布中标结果，但是离场的双方似乎已经心里有数。
t_en: The result of the Commercial District bid is not announced, but both parties leave with a good idea of the outcome.

t_ch: 你看看这个。
t_en: Take a look at this.

t_ch: 你是想随便丢出一件材料，然后等待我的反应吗？
t_en: You can't just present something irrelevant and expect me to respond.

t_ch: 这与我们的讨论无关，请收回吧。
t_en: I can't say what this has to do with our discussions. Please remove it.

t_ch: 你看看这个。
t_en: Take a look at this.

t_ch: 我承认商会在经营码头时赚到了不少，但是跟公司比起来还是杯水车薪。
t_en: I admit that the guild has made a fortune from the dock, but the IPC can offer much more.

t_ch: 为了金人巷商会的颜面，请收回吧。
t_en: You should retract this — I wouldn't want to embarrass the guild.

t_ch: 金人巷商会的各位好啊。
t_en: Members of Aurum Alley Merchant Guild, I hope you're all keeping well.

t_ch: 商会利用码头物流，不仅还上了租金，还筹集到了竞标资金，这足以看出码头的重要性。
t_en: By utilizing the dock, the guild managed to repay outstanding rents and also raised sufficient funding to stand at the bidding. That just goes to show how vital the dock is.

t_ch: 公司愿意更进一步，充分开发码头的物流效应。将金人巷打造成银河闻名的商业港口。
t_en: The IPC seeks to unleash the full potential of the dock, taking full advantage of its role as a logistics center — thus transforming Aurum Alley into one of the most advanced commercial ports in the galaxy.

t_ch: 这难道不是大家所期望的、终极的「金人巷复兴」吗？
t_en: And that, honorable ladies and gentlemen, is the rejuvenation that everyone has been hoping for.

t_ch: 「罗浮第一夜市」？在公司注资后，仙舟罗浮上处处都会建起新的商业中心，人们为什么会专程来到你的金人巷呢？他们早就不在意谁才是「第一夜市」了。
t_en: "Best night market on the Luofu"? With the IPC's funding pouring in, hundreds of commercial centers will sprout. Why would people come to Aurum Alley when they have so many other options? Nobody cares about that title anymore.

t_ch: 金人巷需要在历史的演进中抓住机遇，转变自己的职责。「金人巷」在公司选择的未来里，会成为最有分量的名字。
t_en: Aurum Alley needs to adapt and reimagine itself. The future that the IPC offers will ensure its name is known in the most remote corners of the galaxy.

t_ch: 抱歉，我有些发呆。
t_en: Sorry, I was zoning out.

t_ch: 我听说，小少爷游学归来之后，比过去沉稳了不少。想必是那段在外的经历，让他得出了现在的思考。
t_en: I'd heard that Xiaohan had matured a lot since returning from his study abroad. It would appear the time he spent outside the Xianzhou really did make him a better person.

t_ch: 经历会改变一个人。
t_en: We change as we live.

t_ch: 我们又何尝不是呢？
t_en: I've matured as well, right?

t_ch: 您说的没有错。
t_en: You're right.

t_ch: 我正在想，尽管现在我们对商会的决策无比确信，但事实上，我们还是囿于经历与眼界。
t_en: It really got me thinking... Even though it seems like we're doing the right thing, will we still think so ten years from now?

t_ch: …不好意思，耽误您太久，我先回码头了。
t_en: ...Sorry, you don't have to listen to me daydreaming. I'll head back to the dock for now.

t_ch: 你不会觉得商会肯定中标了吧？
t_en: Do you think the guild won the bid?

t_ch: 那当然啊，我们可是主人公。
t_en: Of course, we're the protagonists.

t_ch: 自称主人公可还行。
t_en: It sure feels like it sometimes...

t_ch: 怎么想都是公司赢定了吧？
t_en: Surely the IPC will win?

t_ch: 我倒没有这么悲观……
t_en: I'm not that pessimistic...

t_ch: 现在去爆破公司还来得及吗？
t_en: Is it too late to blow up the IPC?

t_ch: 晚了晚了，已经来不及了。
t_en: Not the brightest idea.

t_ch: 我才不要什么都不做干等着，我先回金人巷了，再会！
t_en: Anyway, no point in standing around here worrying. I'm heading back to the Alley. Talk to you soon!

t_ch: 你们来了，你们二位是被我生拉硬拽带来的帮手，我应该专程向你们道谢。
t_en: Ah, you're here. I'm the one who dragged you into all this — I must thank you again.

t_ch: 感谢你们为金人巷和金人巷商会所做的一切。
t_en: Thank you for everything you've done for the guild and the Alley.

t_ch: 你太客气了。
t_en: That's too kind of you.

t_ch: 没错，你太客气了。
t_en: Yeah, all in a day's work.

t_ch: 两位对金人巷的帮助居功至伟，这是应当的。
t_en: You two have done a lot to help us. You deserve every bit of praise.

t_ch: 我已经拿到不少奖励了。
t_en: Fancy giving us some more rewards?

t_ch: 对，{F#她}{M#他}从不会客气的。
t_en: {F#She}{M#He} meant to say "thank you very much."

t_ch: 两位对金人巷的帮助居功至伟，这是应当的。
t_en: You two have done a lot to help us. You deserve every bit of praise.

t_ch: 最近闲了下来，有很多事情才来得及思考。
t_en: Now that I've finally got some free time, I'm starting to reflect on the choices I made and the reasons behind them.

t_ch: 会长的经历塑造了会长的判断，小少爷的经历塑造了小少爷的判断，我们又何尝不是？
t_en: The president and Xiaohan had different perspectives because they came from different backgrounds... and I can't help but feel the decisions I made were somewhat restricted by my own background.

t_ch: 每个人都是如此。
t_en: The same can be said for all of us.

t_ch: 我想既然经历如此重要，我应该多去经历。金人巷复兴的方法既在金人巷里，也在金人巷之外，我不能一直停留在这里。
t_en: If that's true, the only way to get a wider perspective is to leave my comfort zone and look outward. The answer to reviving Aurum Alley isn't necessarily within the Alley itself...

t_ch: 于是我拜托了小少爷，他帮我安排了去往庇尔波因特的实习机会，不久就准备出发了。
t_en: ...and that's why I secured an internship opportunity at Pier Point with the help of Xiaohan. Once I wrap up here, I'll be on my way.

t_ch: 开阔眼界是好事。
t_en: It's always good to broaden one's mind.

t_ch: 你走了，金人巷怎么办呢？
t_en: Who'll look after the Alley while you're gone?

t_ch: 我想拜托两位，在我离开期间帮忙照顾打理金人巷。会长会代替我协助你们的。
t_en: I plan to entrust the Alley to you two. If you ever need any help, just ask the president.

t_ch: 没错，你们要是想继续「码放货物」和完成「物流规划」，就来码头找我吧。
t_en: That's right. If you wish to continue with the inventory or logistics planning, come and find me.

t_ch: 哦，你在这里呢。
t_en: Oh, didn't see you there.

t_ch: 我将暂时告别这里，希望我能学成归来，各位再见。
t_en: I hope I can come back with new insights. Bye for now, I'll see you when I get back.

t_ch: 突然有一种「事情告一段落」的感觉了。
t_en: Guess we can say "that's it for today's episode."

t_ch: 想起来，最开始我只是想「日行一善、急人所急」而已，没想到，我们竟然做了这么多……
t_en: "Be eager to help those in need and do one good deed a day..." I like to practice what I preach, but I never thought we'd accomplish so much.

t_ch: 要不是你和斯科特打赌…
t_en: All thanks to your bet with Skott...

t_ch: 我总不能在那个时候退缩呀，再说了，我们不是赢了嘛。
t_en: I couldn't have backed down, could I? Besides, we got him good.

t_ch: 要不是你的嗓门比较大…
t_en: All thanks to your shouting match with Skott...

t_ch: 我听白露说你们老远就听到我的声音了，真的吗？
t_en: Bailu told me you two heard me yelling before you showed up. Is that true?

t_ch: 要不是白露喜欢凑热闹…
t_en: All thanks to Bailu looking for trouble...

t_ch: 我其实也是发现码头闹事才去凑热闹的。
t_en: I don't blame her. I got excited when I heard people arguing at the dock... and the rest is history.

t_ch: 现在回过神来，我总感觉金人巷太幸运了，不知道是不是我的错觉？
t_en: It feels surreal that everything worked out for the Alley.

t_ch: 你是觉得这一切都不真实？
t_en: Pretty crazy.

t_ch: 确实，金人巷也太幸运了。
t_en: Indeed. The Alley got lucky.

t_ch: 难道不是我们太厉害了吗？
t_en: Things worked out because of our hard work.

t_ch: 我当然知道我们很厉害…我还是觉得金人巷很幸运。
t_en: Yeah... Everything could have easily gone wrong.

t_ch: 虽然有过磕磕碰碰，有过互不信任，但争吵和矛盾竟然都是为了金人巷能变好…这个过程中只要有人成心使坏，我们都不会像现在这样顺利。
t_en: Sure, there were clashes and distrust, but virtually everyone was acting in the best interest of the Alley. If that hadn't been the case, the whole thing would have fallen apart.

t_ch: 啊…斯科特不算，那个坏家伙费尽心思搞破坏，反倒显得像一个缺心眼。
t_en: I say "virtually" because of Skott, of course... Lucky for us, he wasn't as smart as he thought he was.

t_ch: 小手段无法获得真正的认可。
t_en: Cheaters won't prosper.

t_ch: 是呀，驭空大人不会对破坏商业秩序的行为坐视不理的。不等我们出手，天舶司也会派人来处理，斯科特这样的人肯定得意不了。
t_en: Exactly. Madam Yukong would never allow malicious competition. If we hadn't taught Skott a lesson, the Sky-Faring Commission would have done so anyway.

t_ch: 如果斯科特是小少爷的上司…
t_en: If Skott has turned out to be Xiaohan's superior...

t_ch: 那么公司可能就会更加肆无忌惮，更加难对付…幸好小少爷是一个讲道理的人。
t_en: Then the IPC would've been more brutal, and the situation would've been much harder for us... Thankfully, Xiaohan turned out to be upright and reasonable.

t_ch: 所以我在想，这样的幸运是理所应当的吗，金人巷走到现在这一步是巧合吗？
t_en: Taking all that into account, did we get lucky, or was Aurum Alley always destined to pull through?

t_ch: 有幸运，有巧合，但无关紧要。
t_en: Not sure... but I don't think it matters.

t_ch: 对，我也是这样想的！
t_en: Agreed!

t_ch: 没想到素裳居然考虑了这么多？
t_en: It was all thanks to my literary mind.

t_ch: 什么嘛，我书读得少，但是我娘给我讲过的道理可不少！
t_en: Hmph, I may not be as well-read as you, but my mom taught me a thing or two!

t_ch: 我想肯定是因为「热爱金人巷的情感」，所以不同的人才会因「热爱」汇聚于此，才会出现不合理的「幸运与巧合」，对吧？
t_en: I guess the love and care for the Alley brought everyone together, and that's why despite all the difficulties, things turned out for the best.

t_ch: 素裳说得对呀。
t_en: Well said, Sushang.

t_ch: 嘿嘿嘿。
t_en: Hehe.

t_ch: 很不习惯你讲大道理的样子。
t_en: Never heard so many wise words from you before.

t_ch: 哼，现在开始习惯吧。
t_en: Ha, better start getting used to it.

t_ch: 虽然感慨也感慨得足够了，但金人巷还没有完成复兴呢…码头的事情还得麻烦你。
t_en: Monologues aside, there's still plenty of work to do to get the Alley back on track. The dock still needs your help.

t_ch: 要是遇到什么其他困难，记得随时叫上我，我就先忙去啦～
t_en: Let me know if you run into trouble again, I'll be there. Talk to you later!

t_ch: 斯科特对你的出现视而不见，当然，你也可以对他不屑。
t_en: Skott chooses to ignore your presence, and in return, you can choose to disdain him.

t_ch: 你曾经在天舶司见过夕葵小姐，出于公正的原则，她不会在这个时候与你私聊。
t_en: You may have met Miss Xikui at the Sky-Faring Commission, but due to her commitment to impartiality, she refrains from engaging in private conversations with you at the moment.

t_ch: 开摆！
t_en: Let's get started!

t_ch: 少说废话了，让我来吧。
t_en: Enough of that. Let me do it.

t_ch: 现在就开始规划吧。
t_en: Let's get the planning started.

t_ch: 让我试试，不行再换人。
t_en: Let me try. If that doesn't work, put someone else in.

t_ch: 试试就试试！
t_en: Couldn't hurt, I guess!

t_ch: 让我连线，我现在就要连线。
t_en: I love planning stuff. I wanna do it now!

t_ch: 我叫阿丰，是我将那个带有「浓云金蟾」的箱子带出来的。
t_en: My name's A-Feng — I was the one who brought out the box containing the Golden Cloud Toad.

t_ch: 别人叫我搬，我就搬了，我也没想到里面装着故障机巧，这件事和我没有关系。
t_en: I was told to move something, and I did. I had no knowledge of the presence of an ingenium. This matter has nothing to do with me.

t_ch: 我也是码头工人，我怎么可能向着星际和平公司呢……
t_en: I'm a dock worker, after all. How could I conspire with the IPC?

t_ch: 我叫阿丰，是我将那个带有「浓云金蟾」的箱子带出来的。
t_en: My name's A-Feng — I was the one who brought out the box containing the Golden Cloud Toad.

t_ch: 别人叫我搬，我就搬了，我也没想到里面装着故障机巧，这件事和我没有关系。
t_en: I was told to move something, and I did. I had no knowledge of the presence of an ingenium. This matter has nothing to do with me.

t_ch: 我也是码头工人，我怎么可能向着星际和平公司呢……
t_en: I'm a dock worker, after all. How could I conspire with the IPC?

t_ch: 你察觉到阿丰紧张地避开视线，他的话里好像有什么问题……
t_en: A-Feng was being shifty and avoidant, there seems to be something off about the way he's speaking...

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 我没有看到什么箱子，只看到了「浓云金蟾」。
t_en: I didn't see any boxes, only the Golden Cloud Toad.

t_ch: 你们要检查货物，我们当然就把货箱拆了，不然，你要自己拆吗？
t_en: If goods need to be inspected, we remove the packaging ahead of time — you wouldn't want to do all that work yourself, would you?

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 你为什么强调这事和自己没有关系？
t_en: Why do you emphasize that this matter has nothing to do with you?

t_ch: 我不是怕你误会嘛，就好像是我故意放进去的一样。
t_en: I just want to avoid any misunderstanding, in case someone has it in their head that I intentionally misplaced an item...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>（这句话好像不太对劲……）</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>(I sense a hole in his logic...)</color>

t_ch: 出示
t_en: (Present evidence)

t_ch: 你不可能和这件事没关系。
t_en: I think this has a lot to do with you...

t_ch: 鹤运物流上有记录显示，这箱快递就是「阿丰」寄出去的，你怎么说？
t_en: Records from Heron Express show that this container was sent by a person named "A-Feng." What do you have to say about that?

t_ch: 啊，这个，会不会是哪里搞错了呢？
t_en: Ah! Th—That... must be some kind of mistake.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 码头工人就必须和星际和平公司对立吗？
t_en: Does being a dock worker mean that person can't conspire with the IPC?

t_ch: 谁不知道他们想要利用码头债务，让商会彻底失去信誉嘛。商会雇佣了我，又不是公司雇佣了我，我不可能支持公司的。
t_en: Everyone knows the IPC wants to use the dock debt to discredit the Merchant Guild. The Merchant Guild are the ones who gave me a job, not the IPC — why would I side with them?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>（这句话好像不太对劲……）</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>(I sense a hole in his logic...)</color>

t_ch: 出示
t_en: (Present evidence)

t_ch: 你看看这个。
t_en: Take a look at this.

t_ch: 你收到了公司的信用点，为了这点好处，你帮助公司破坏码头的运输活动。
t_en: You received payments from the IPC, and in exchange you sabotaged the shipping operations at the dock.

t_ch: 出示
t_en: (Present evidence)

t_ch: 你看看这个。
t_en: Take a look at this.

t_ch: 这和现在的话题无关吧。
t_en: This has nothing to do with the topic at hand.

t_ch: 金人巷越来越繁华……
t_en: The Aurum Alley is getting more and more flourishing...

t_ch: 朋友，我在码头上见过你。你代表商会和不少商户建立了合作，其实我也是做小本生意的，金人巷码头的福利能不能也让我沾沾光？
t_en: Friend, I saw you at the docks before. You rep a guild and have connections throughout the business world. Y'know, I'm something of a businessman myself. Can I get in on some of this Aurum Alley dock's hustle?

t_ch: 当然可以，我帮你联系小秘书。
t_en: Of course. I'll put you in touch with the secretary.

t_ch: 诶，不急不急，我们先谈，我们聊完了再找小秘书也不迟……
t_en: Hey, no rush. Let's just chat for now, and if we want to link up with the secretary after that, then it'll be fine...

t_ch: 那么，你的店在哪里呢？
t_en: So, where's your store?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，朋友，思维要打开，我走到哪，我的店就开在哪。
t_en: Haha, my friend, have an open mind. Wheresoever I go, there you shall find my store.

t_ch: 不过嘛…我这个店卖的东西，多少都有点见不得光…朋友，你懂吧？我特地来找你先商量商量，看看这个事能不能成。
t_en: But, ah... The things I sell in this store of mine, they're kinda shady... My friend, know what I'm saying? I came here just to talk with you first about whether this could happen.

t_ch: 我懂了，你是黑市商人。
t_en: I know what you mean. You're a black market dealer.

t_ch: 诶，怎么说话的！我喜欢称自己为「特殊物品经销商」。
t_en: Hey, what are you saying? I prefer to think of myself as a "Special Arms Wholesaler."

t_ch: 你完了，我报警了。
t_en: You're done for. I'm reporting you to the authorities.

t_ch: 哟，哟，哟！
t_en: Yo yo yo...!

t_ch: 你要开始说唱了？
t_en: Tryna bust out a freestyle rap, are we?

t_ch: 我踩你脚了？
t_en: Did I step on your foot?

t_ch: 你触电了？
t_en: Are you being electrocuted?

t_ch: 你看，误会了不是？朋友，我这也是正经生意，有照经营……
t_en: Look, this must be a misunderstanding, right? My friend, I'm just an honorable businessman, with a business license and everything...

t_ch: 现在罗浮处处都查得严，我这不是不方便嘛…但是呢，我有个客人急需一批药材，朋友，咱这码头物流是不是能帮个忙？
t_en: The authorities are cracking down everywhere on the Luofu. It's a real pain... But you know, I have a customer who urgently needs some of my medicine. My friend, can you help me out with the dockside logistics here?

t_ch: 什么药材，符合仙舟法律吗？
t_en: What medicine? Is it legal per Xianzhou law?

t_ch: 朋友放心吧，「法无禁止即可为」…咱们这儿卖的东西，就刚刚好卡在那个「法无禁止」的范围内。
t_en: My friend, don't worry. "Anything not explicitly prohibited is technically legal"... All this good stuff lies right in that "not explicitly prohibited" area.

t_ch: 可以，跟我去找小秘书吧。
t_en: Fine. Follow me. We're off to see the secretary.

t_ch: 别别别，你要给那个小姑娘说了，咱这批货是要留下痕迹的…这不太好。
t_en: No no no, you have to tell that little girl that this batch of ours is going to leave behind traces... That's no good.

t_ch: 码头只要长期合作，不接私活。
t_en: We're not taking on shady gigs. Legit business only.

t_ch: 朋友，你这样说就有些不信任我了。
t_en: My friend, you don't sound like you trust me when you say that.

t_ch: 我是支持你们金人巷商会的，你看我没有店面，走走停停，公司是不会来找我谈合作的，更别说什么赔偿了。
t_en: I support your Aurum Alley Merchant Guild. You see, I don't have a storefront of my own. I mosey about on my way, but the IPC won't come and negotiate any deals with me — let alone mention any money.

t_ch: 再说了，要是以后这里彻底被公司接管了，肯定也没有我的一席之地了。我可以与商会建立正式的合作，不过嘛，需要一个建立信任的过程。
t_en: Besides, there's no place for someone like me once the IPC swallows up this place. I can draw up a formal treaty with a guild, but you see, it requires a process of building trust.

t_ch: 这样吧，你帮我送一件货物，要是送到了，你回来找我，我们谈合作如何？
t_en: What about this? You help me deliver a package and if it gets through, you come back to me. And then we can talk about working together. How's that?

t_ch: 我才不给你当枪使。
t_en: I won't be your pawn.

t_ch: 别啊！我霍三哥虽然不算什么好东西，但绝对算是个说话算话的生意人！
t_en: No! They may not call me "Good Guy Mr. Huo," but I'm absolutely a trader who keeps his word!

t_ch: 交给我吧。
t_en: Leave it to me.

t_ch: 嘿！就喜欢你这样的敞亮人！放心好了，等你回来，我一定不会亏待你的！
t_en: Hey! I like open-minded people like you! Don't worry. You'll be handsomely rewarded when you get back.

t_ch: 你拿着我改造好的机巧鸟过去，将快递放到一个高处——我给你在导航里标记好了，客人会自己来取的。
t_en: Take the modded cycrane with you and send it to a very high location. I'll put a marker on your navigator so the client can collect.

t_ch: 不过朋友，我信任你，你可千万别打开「货物」，也别问客人是谁，千万记住。
t_en: But friend, I trust you. You must never, ever open the "goods." And never ask about the client, either. Don't forget that.

t_ch: （要不就在这里用机巧鸟把霍三哥的「货物」送上去吧……）
t_en: (Why not use the cycrane to send Mr. Huo's "goods" straight from here...)

t_ch: 喂，你手上的是「霍三哥的货」吧？
t_en: Hey, is that "Mr. Huo's goods" you've got over there?

t_ch: 辛苦你这么远送过来了，就把「货物」放在这里吧。
t_en: Thanks for bringing it all this way. Put those "goods" over here then.

t_ch: 霍三哥可没说有客人自提…
t_en: Mr. Huo never mentioned anything about a collection...

t_ch: 霍三哥没告诉你的事多了，放下吧。
t_en: Mr. Huo never mentioned a lot of things to you, kid. Just hand it over.

t_ch: 你们云骑军要「霍三哥的货」？
t_en: A Cloud Knight wants "Mr. Huo's goods"?

t_ch: 啊，没错，不行吗？
t_en: Uh, yeah. Why not?

t_ch: 不行，我坚持用机巧鸟送上去。
t_en: No way. I insist on sending via a cycrane.

t_ch: 你是不是听不懂我说的话，放下吧。
t_en: Do you even understand the words I am saying to you? Hand it over.

t_ch: 我不想为难你，你把东西放下转身离开，否则，别怪我手段强硬了。
t_en: I don't want to trouble you, so just put the "goods" down, turn around, and walk away. Otherwise, don't blame me if I start playing hardball.

t_ch: （照他说的，放下霍三哥的货）
t_en: (Do as he says, and put down Mr. Huo's goods)

t_ch: 现在你可以离开这里了。
t_en: You can get outta here now.

t_ch: （再仔细观察一下眼前的云骑）
t_en: (Take a closer look at this Cloud Knight before you)

t_ch: 看什么看，没见过云骑军吗？
t_en: What are you looking at? Never seen a Cloud Knight before?

t_ch: 仔细一看，你不是云骑士兵。
t_en: Wait a sec, you are no Cloud Knight!

t_ch: 你为什么知道我有货呢？
t_en: How do you know I have the goods?

t_ch: 我讨厌你这种直觉敏锐的快递员。
t_en: I hate cunning couriers like you.

t_ch: （霍三哥要求我不能打开货物，也不能问客人是谁？）
t_en: (Mr. Huo has forbidden me from opening up the "goods," and from asking who the client is?)

t_ch: （换句话来说，我很难验证那位「云骑」到底是真是假…）
t_en: (So in other words, it's difficult for me to verify whether or not that "Cloud Knight" is the real deal...)

t_ch: （霍三哥只说让我用机巧鸟将货物放于高处…这人可能有问题。该怎么做？）
t_en: (All Mr. Huo said was for me to use this cycrane to send the "goods" to a very high location... This guy seems suspicious, though. What to do?)

t_ch: （不行，我还得再去问问他）
t_en: (No, I have to ask him again)

t_ch: （想太多了，干脆直接离开这里）
t_en: (I'm overthinking this. Just get out of here, right now)

t_ch: 你怎么又回来了？
t_en: What are you doing back here?

t_ch: 仔细一看，你不是云骑士兵。
t_en: Wait a sec, you are no Cloud Knight!

t_ch: 你为什么知道我有货呢？
t_en: How do you know I have the goods?

t_ch: 我还有些问题想问你。
t_en: I still have a few things to ask you about...

t_ch: 我讨厌你这种直觉敏锐的快递员。
t_en: I hate cunning couriers like you.

t_ch: 你站住，我还有些话要说。
t_en: You, stop. I still have some words to say.

t_ch: 是你让我走的，现在又反悔了？
t_en: You let me go, and now you're trying to back out of it?

t_ch: 我反悔了。
t_en: I'm reneging on it.

t_ch: 刚才我就觉得你有问题，说吧。
t_en: I was just thinking about how suspicious you seemed. So go ahead.

t_ch: 你真的觉得有问题，就不是我来找你了。
t_en: You really think I'm suspicious? If that's so, I would not have come here looking for you.

t_ch: 长官，我真的只是来送货的。
t_en: Captain, I'm really just here to deliver the "goods."

t_ch: 我知道，你真是来送货的。
t_en: I know. you're really just here to deliver the "goods."

t_ch: …等着，你听我说完。
t_en: ...Wait a minute. Hear me out.

t_ch: 你这个人也太容易信任别人了，穿着「云骑」衣服的，难道就是「云骑」吗？
t_en: You are too trusting of other people. Is everyone wearing the "Cloud Knight" armor in fact a "Cloud Knight"?

t_ch: 我让你放下，你就放下，甚至就这样离开，难道你没有丝毫怀疑吗？
t_en: I told you to let it go and you just did it. You even just left like that — didn't you have the slightest of suspicions?

t_ch: 我不忍心就这样欺骗你，告诉你吧，我是被雇来考验你的人，不是「云骑」。
t_en: I can't bear to trick you like this, so let me tell you: I've been hired to test you, and I'm not a real "Cloud Knight."

t_ch: 好感动，原来你是这么好的人？
t_en: I'm touched. I guess you really are a good person after all?

t_ch: 别感动了，你要从这次经验中学到教训啊。
t_en: Don't be touched. You have to learn something from this lesson.

t_ch: 什么？！你竟然骗了我！
t_en: What!? You dare try and cheat me?

t_ch: 是你自己没有警惕意识，你应该多听听地衡司的反诈骗讲座。
t_en: It's you who wasn't vigilant enough. You should pay more attention to those anti-scam ads from the Realm-Keeping Commission.

t_ch: 我早知道了，想偷个懒而已。
t_en: I knew that all along. I just didn't care.

t_ch: 你怎么这样啊？别人拜托你的事，你应该负责到底呀。
t_en: The nerve of some people... When people ask you to do something, you should assume a modicum of responsibility.

t_ch: 来吧，拿回你的「货物」，做完你该做的事。
t_en: C'mon, take back your "goods" and finish what you were meant to do.

t_ch: （莫名其妙……）
t_en: (Beyond words...)

t_ch: （总之，还得用机巧鸟把货物送到目标地点。）
t_en: (Anyway, I still have to use the cycrane to deliver the "goods" to their intended destination.)

t_ch: （好，「货物」顺利送到了。回去和霍三哥说一声吧。）
t_en: (Alright, the "goods" have been delivered. Go back and talk to Mr. Huo.)

t_ch: （完成一处，还有下一处。）
t_en: (Onto the next spot.)

t_ch: （接下来要把「货物」放在霍三哥定好的位置上，自会有人来取。）
t_en: (I should place the goods where Mr. Huo wants them. Someone will be here for collection.)

t_ch: 「货物」已到达指定位置，正在投放，请勿移动……
t_en: The goods have arrived at their designated location. Placement in process. Please do not move...

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，{NICKNAME}你回来了，我收到客人的信息，你已经准确将货物送到了。这一路上没出问题吧？
t_en: Hahaha, {NICKNAME}! You're back! I heard from the client that you've delivered the goods as requested. Any problems along the way?

t_ch: 轻松愉快。
t_en: Easy-peasy.

t_ch: 我明白了，你擅长举重若轻。我知道你帮我送货的时候，遇到了小小麻烦。
t_en: I get it. You're a very capable one. I know I put you through the ringer with delivering the goods.

t_ch: 但是你为了帮助我偷运货物，击退了云骑军，真是太靠谱啦。
t_en: But you even fought off the Cloud Knights just to help me smuggle some goods. You really are reliable!

t_ch: 有云骑军想要带走货物。
t_en: There was a Cloud Knight who wanna grab the goods.

t_ch: 朋友你为了帮助我偷运货物，击退了云骑军，真是太靠谱啦。
t_en: My friend, you even fought off the Cloud Knights just to help me smuggle some goods. You really are reliable!

t_ch: 你尽管放心，那位不是真的云骑，神策府不会找你的麻烦，而是我雇来的「演员」。
t_en: Don't worry. That person wasn't a real Cloud Knight, so the Seat of Divine Foresight won't be looking for you. They were just an actor I hired.

t_ch: 没想到，你竟然考验我？
t_en: I didn't expect you to test me.

t_ch: 说到底你还是不信任我。
t_en: So, basically you don't trust me, huh.

t_ch: 做我们这行的，最重要的就是谨慎。你放心朋友，你通过了我的考验，取得了我的信任，我说到做到。
t_en: In this line of business, the most important thing is being cautious. Relax, my friend. You've passed my test and won my trust. I will do as I say.

t_ch: 我会以「个体经销商」的身份与你合作，我向你承诺，码头会有源源不断的运单需求，不过它们都会有「正当的名义」，都来自金人巷的商铺。
t_en: I'll cooperate with you as a "sole distributor." I promise you that the docks will be drowning in an unending stream of orders, with each of these orders being "legal" and coming from the shops of Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 有点吓人，什么情况？
t_en: Sounds scary. What's the situation?

t_ch: 放心老爷，都是正规正经的货物。
t_en: Relax, these are perfectly authentic goods.

t_ch: 那些商铺们知道吗？
t_en: Do the shops know?

t_ch: 这个嘛，我建议您不要问，我只能说都是合规合法的，我向您保证。
t_en: Well, you know... I recommend that you don't ask that. All I can assure you is that they're all legal. You have my word on that.

t_ch: 当然，这么多订单也不是凭空变出来的，老爷您可以在我这里买一些「昂贵的配方」，我们有来有往，实现双赢，这样如何？
t_en: Of course, all these orders don't come out of nowhere. You can buy some "costly remedies" from me. A little business between us will be to our mutual profit. How about it?

t_ch: 请尽可能遵守法律。
t_en: Please obey the law as much as possible.

t_ch: 一定一定，我做了这么久生意，最懂遵守法律了。
t_en: Of course, of course. I'm a business veteran, you know. Following the law is my forte.

t_ch: 祝你发财。
t_en: I wish you great success.

t_ch: 咱俩都是响当当的生意人，早晚都能发大财！
t_en: You and me, we're both established business people. Sooner or later we'll strike it big!

t_ch: 您慢走，以后要是有什么事，还要仰赖朋友你帮忙呀。
t_en: Farewell then. If anything comes up later, I'll be relying on your support, my friend.

t_ch: 阿姨听金人巷商会的小秘书提起过你，你们商会要用金人巷码头把货发到其他洞天里去。
t_en: I heard about you from Miss Secretary of the Merchant Guild. The Guild plans to use the Aurum Alley dock to ship goods to other delves.

t_ch: 小秘书给我说，希望美馔阁也能做一些外卖生意——商务活动餐盒、旅行餐盒、又或是那些不方便出门的客人想吃咱家的饭了，也能出外卖。
t_en: Miss Secretary told me that she was hoping Delicacy Pavilion could start making takeouts — things like business meals and travel lunch boxes. Customers who don't have time to eat out would have the option to have the food delivered to them.

t_ch: 这多好，我也想吃您家的外卖。
t_en: Sounds perfect to me. I'd love a good takeout meal from Delicacy Pavilion.

t_ch: 谢谢你这么捧场，阿姨是打算做外卖的，只是我有些其他的请求……
t_en: Thank you! Starting the takeout business is indeed on my priority list. It's just...

t_ch: 难道说，阿姨还有什么疑虑吗？
t_en: Are there any particular concerns?

t_ch: 谈不上是疑虑吧，阿姨就是有一些小小的请求……
t_en: I wouldn't call them concerns, more like a small request.

t_ch: 我们美馔阁也算是金人巷美食街的老字号了，但是游客们提起金人巷美食却总是提起「尚滋味」，你想想阿姨这要是做了外卖，可不就……
t_en: You see, Delicacy Pavilion has been around for a while, but when people talk about food in Aurum Alley, they think of Spices Supreme. If I were to start this whole takeout business...

t_ch: 「可不就得有人买吗」。
t_en: You'd need a better reputation.

t_ch: 没错，我想要是做外卖还没有口碑，我多亏呀。
t_en: Exactly. I can't simply jump in only to find people are not even interested.

t_ch: 可以雇点水军刷好评。
t_en: Ever thought about using bots to get good reviews?

t_ch: 不玩虚的。东西好不好吃，看月销量就能看出来了……
t_en: That won't do. Good reviews mean nothing if our monthly sales figures can't back them up.

t_ch: 而且这招阿姨以前用过呀…水军只给你刷些什么「敏感肌也能用」，搞笑吧？
t_en: Besides, I did try the method you mentioned... But the bots wrote comments like "Specially made for sensitive skin!" They didn't even bother to customize a bit!

t_ch: 唉，我就是担心我们要是做了外卖，还不能做出名声，就浪费了这好机会。
t_en: See the problem? If we did start making takeouts and ended up not very successful, it'd be a waste of time and money.

t_ch: 我认为孽物不太能做成食物。
t_en: I don't think abominations should be used as food ingredients.

t_ch: 总得试试嘛，可惜阿姨是朵娇花，没可能打赢孽物。要不您受累帮我带几只回来？
t_en: Gotta think outside the box! Shame that I'm not much of a fighter myself. Would you do me a favor and bring me a few abominations?

t_ch: 矮阿姨，您真是好身手啊！
t_en: You've got some moves!

t_ch: 阿姨是朵娇花，没可能打赢孽物。所以，这不是在拜托{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}你嘛。
t_en: I'm not much of a fighter myself, so I stand no chance against the abominations. Would you mind defeating them in my stead, {F#young lady}{M#young man}?

t_ch: 我听说云骑军截获了一批放生帮偷偷装箱的豺狼，反正都会被处理的，要是能猎回一些，我试试做成菜品…它们应该就在迴星港里，拜托你了。
t_en: I heard the Cloud Knights intercepted a bunch of wolves that the Unshackled was smuggling around. Would be a pity if they just got disposed of. If you can get me some, I'll try coming up with some new menu items. You should be able to find the wolves at Stargazer Navalia. I'm counting on you!

t_ch: 我听说迴星港集装箱里有些豺狼，要是能做道菜，肯定是不错的，你能帮我猎些回来吗？
t_en: I heard rumors that some wolves were found in the containers at Stargazer Navalia. I bet I can come up with some new menu items with them. Can you bring back a few for me?

t_ch: 阿姨打算效仿燕翠师傅研究新菜的做法，去打几只孽物，看看能不能下菜。
t_en: Seeing how Yancui expands her menu, I plan to hunt a few abominations and see what I can do with them.

t_ch: （留在这里也是隐患，尽早处理了吧。）
t_en: (I should take care of them before people get hurt.)

t_ch: 怎么样有收获吗？
t_en: How was it? Learn anything?

t_ch: 好怪！好怪的味道！——呜呃，我还是找个地方把它扔掉吧……
t_en: Ugh... Oh dear, it smells so funky... Better throw it away in a trashcan.

t_ch: 难道说我的辛苦都白费了？
t_en: Are you telling me I did all that for nothing?

t_ch: 不好意思呀，我实在很难把这东西当作是食物…食物，到底是什么来着？
t_en: Sorry. I really am having trouble thinking of this as food... Food. What's that again?

t_ch: 这种话不要当着我的面说呀…
t_en: Don't say that kind of thing around me...

t_ch: 不好意思呀，街坊们都说我说话直，你也别往心里去。
t_en: Sorry. The neighbors all say I'm too blunt. Please don't take it to heart.

t_ch: 燕翠师傅都能处理，你也能！
t_en: Master Yancui can handle it. So can you!

t_ch: 你不要高看阿姨嘞，燕翠师傅是有点功夫在身上的，我就算了吧。
t_en: Don't put me on a pedestal. Yancui is supremely talented. Me? Not so much.

t_ch: 看到这孽物腐肉的瞬间，我脑子里闪过了一些灵感，也不能算是一无所获。可是与此同时，我也受到了精神创伤，可能需要休息一下。
t_en: The moment I saw this abomination, a flash of inspiration struck me and that's not nothing. But then again, I was also traumatized and might just need a rest.

t_ch: 虽然是不情之请…{NICKNAME}你能再帮我一个忙吗？麻烦你在店里跑跑堂，问问客人的点餐需求，告诉后厨就成。
t_en: If I may be so bold as to ask... {NICKNAME}, can you please do me one more favor? Please run around the store and take all the customers' orders, then pass those on to the kitchen.

t_ch: 矮阿姨尽管说，我会帮忙的。
t_en: Whatever you want, Short Auntie. I'm here to help.

t_ch: 太好了，那我就能稍微缓缓了。
t_en: That's great. Then I can take it a little easy.

t_ch: 你们这里都没有扫码点餐的吗？
t_en: Don't you use scanning codes to place orders here?

t_ch: 美馔阁现在还没有…我答应你，小店以后会支持的。现在还得麻烦你。
t_en: Delicacy Pavilion doesn't use that... I promise you, we'll get it later. So right now it's still all up to you.

t_ch: 很简单的，你只要记住客人点的餐、分量，以及忌口就成。
t_en: It's simple, really — you just have to remember the dishes, portion size, and things to avoid for every order.

t_ch: …我真的得去休息了，头晕晕，好像要看到什么幻觉了…你要是准备好了告诉我一声。
t_en: I should get some rest. The world spins... Guess it won't take long before hallucination kicks in. Let me know when you are ready.

t_ch: 我已经准备好了。
t_en: I'm ready.

t_ch: 客人们马上就要到了，店里就拜托你了。
t_en: The diners will be here soon, so I'll leave the store with you.

t_ch: 稍等一下，我还有别的事。
t_en: Wait a minute. I've got something else I need to do.

t_ch: 没事，等你有空的时候。
t_en: It's fine, whenever you're free.

t_ch: 你来了，跑堂其实很简单的，你只要记住客人点的餐、分量，以及忌口就成。
t_en: You're here. Waiting is easy, really — you just have to remember the dishes, portion size, and things to avoid for every order.

t_ch: …我真的得去休息了，头晕晕，好像要看到什么幻觉了…你要是准备好了告诉我一声。
t_en: I should get some rest. The world spins... Guess it won't take long before hallucination kicks in. Let me know when you are ready.

t_ch: 我已经准备好了。
t_en: I'm ready.

t_ch: 客人们马上就要到了，店里就拜托你了。
t_en: The diners will be here soon, so I'll leave the store with you.

t_ch: 稍等一下，我还有别的事。
t_en: Wait a minute. I've got something else I need to do.

t_ch: 没事，等你有空的时候。
t_en: It's fine, whenever you're free.

t_ch: 你怕不是特地来消遣洒家？
t_en: Are you a professional time-waster?

t_ch: 不好意思呀，我实在很难把这东西当作是食材。一股子腥气，冲是冲的来……
t_en: Sorry, I just can't serve this to my customers. So gamey, so intrusive...

t_ch: 可仙舟也没有垃圾桶啊！
t_en: But there are no trashcans on the Xianzhou! ...Trust me, I've checked.

t_ch: 哦哟，{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}说到点子上了。
t_en: Right on point, {F#young lady}{M#young man}!

t_ch: 实在不行的话，阿姨只能把这些东西交给小天了，让她看看能不能试着入药。
t_en: Guess I've no choice but to hand these to Little Tian and see if she can use them for medicine.

t_ch: 哎唷，不妙，这味儿冲得阿姨有些吃不消了。我得到后厨缓缓劲。
t_en: Wait... I think I'm gonna throw up. Excuse me now...

t_ch: 你又要叫我白白跑腿了？
t_en: Running errands for nothing, again?

t_ch: 不白跑，不白跑！阿姨准备了一笔报酬，保证让你觉得这趟辛苦值得嘞。
t_en: Of course not! I'll make it worth your while.

t_ch: 没有电子菜单让客人点吗？
t_en: Can't customers order digitally?

t_ch: 把那些狗皮膏药似的菜单贴在桌上？不不不，咱们得让来这儿的游客体验一下纯正的，古老的饮食文化韵味。
t_en: You mean getting the menu to display on the table? No way. I will ensure an authentic, traditional experience for my customers.

t_ch: （听说过美馔阁的客人不少，但是没想到会有这么多……）
t_en: (I knew Delicacy Pavilion was popular, but I never thought there'd be this many people...)

t_ch: （要是客人们同时点单，真的能记得住吗？）
t_en: (Can I really remember everything if two customers order at the same time?)

t_ch: （尽量记住吧。）
t_en: (I'll just do my best.)

t_ch: 啊，我要一份葱醋鸡、一份月华饭，大碗的。
t_en: Mmm, I'd like one portion of scallion chicken, and one portion of moonlight rice. Large bowls, please.

t_ch: 不好意思，再说一遍？
t_en: I'm sorry. Could you repeat that?

t_ch: 行不行啊，我只讲一遍——我要一份葱醋鸡、一份月华饭，大碗的。
t_en: Okay, I'll say this only once: One portion of scallion chicken, and one portion of moonlight rice. Large bowls, please.

t_ch: 好的，请稍等。
t_en: Okay, one moment please.

t_ch: 来一份肉桂香排盖饭，再来份豉油蒸鸡，不要香菜。
t_en: Gimme one portion of cinnamon pork ribs on rice, and a portion of steamed soy sauce chicken. No coriander.

t_ch: 不好意思，再说一遍？
t_en: I'm sorry. Could you repeat that?

t_ch: 一份肉桂香排盖饭，一份豉油蒸鸡。
t_en: One portion of cinnamon pork ribs on rice, and a portion of steamed soy sauce chicken.

t_ch: 不要香菜，不要香菜，不要香菜，千万记住了。
t_en: No coriander. No coriander. No Coriander. Please don't forget.

t_ch: 好的，请稍等。
t_en: Okay, one moment please.

t_ch: 紫芋扣肉来一份。
t_en: One purple taro with pork.

t_ch: 啊，刚才那个扣肉不要了，给我换成脆皮仔猪，再来点古藤百草饮，中杯。
t_en: Mmm, actually scrap that purple taro with pork. Give me a crispy suckling pig instead, and a medium cup of herbal tea.

t_ch: 不好意思，再说一遍？
t_en: I'm sorry. Could you repeat that?

t_ch: 一份脆皮仔猪，再来点古藤百草饮，中杯。
t_en: One crispy suckling pig. One medium cup of herbal tea.

t_ch: 好的，请稍等。
t_en: Okay, one moment please.

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}，我记得第一位客人点了份葱醋鸡，还有一份什么？
t_en: {F#Girl}{M#Boy}, I remember the first diner wanted a portion of scallion chicken, and a — what else?

t_ch: 中碗月华饭。
t_en: A medium bowl of moonlight rice.

t_ch: 大碗，我点的是大碗。
t_en: Large. I wanted a large bowl.

t_ch: 大碗月华饭。
t_en: A large bowl of moonlight rice.

t_ch: 对，就是这个。
t_en: Yeah, that's it.

t_ch: 超大碗月华饭。
t_en: A mega bowl of moonlight rice.

t_ch: 哪有什么超大碗啊，我点的是大碗。
t_en: What mega bowl? I ordered a large one.

t_ch: 第二位客人点的是肉桂香排盖饭，还有份什么来着？
t_en: The second diner wanted cinnamon pork ribs on rice, and — what else?

t_ch: 豉油蒸鸡，不要鸡。
t_en: Steamed soy sauce chicken, no chicken.

t_ch: 啊？！只要豉油？
t_en: Huh!? Just soy sauce?

t_ch: 我说的是，不要香菜！
t_en: I said, no coriander!

t_ch: 豉油蒸鸡，不要葱姜蒜。
t_en: Steamed soy sauce chicken. No spring onion, no ginger, and no garlic.

t_ch: 你行不行啊，我不要香菜！
t_en: Guys, I said no coriander!

t_ch: 怎么还有人不要香菜的……
t_en: Why do some people hate coriander...

t_ch: 豉油蒸鸡，不要香菜。
t_en: Steamed soy sauce chicken. No coriander.

t_ch: 对，就是这个。
t_en: Yeah, that's it.

t_ch: 第三位客人点的是脆皮仔猪，还有份饮料，是什么来着？
t_en: The third diner wanted crispy suckling pig and a drink of — what was it again?

t_ch: 星芋啵啵，不加冰，两倍糖。
t_en: Startaro bubble tea. No ice, double sugar.

t_ch: 住手，我点的是中杯古藤百草饮。
t_en: Stop. I ordered a medium cup of herbal tea.

t_ch: 龙泉老窖，三大碗。
t_en: Dragon Spring Brew, three large bowls.

t_ch: 住手，我点的是中杯古藤百草饮。
t_en: Stop. I ordered a medium cup of herbal tea.

t_ch: 中杯古藤百草饮。
t_en: A medium cup of herbal tea.

t_ch: 对，就是这个。
t_en: Yeah, that's it.

t_ch: 客人没有点饮料。
t_en: They didn't order a drink.

t_ch: 喂！我点的是中杯古藤百草饮。
t_en: Hey! I ordered a medium cup of herbal tea.

t_ch: 忙碌了半晌，你切身体会到了金人巷小吃街游人如织，往来不绝。
t_en: After a long period of hard work, you can personally feel that Aurum Alley is indeed pumping with throngs of tourists.

t_ch: 辛苦你了，我听说后厨说你完全没有点错单，真是太厉害了。
t_en: Thanks for your hard work. I heard the chef say that you didn't mess up any orders at all, which was really amazing.

t_ch: 辛苦你了，今天的客人很多呢，一个人应付那么多事，肯定很不容易。
t_en: Thanks for your hard work. There were loads of diners today. It's really hard for one person to do everything you did.

t_ch: 刚才，矮阿姨已经同意与码头建立合作了。那就麻烦{NICKNAME}将「美馔阁」纳入到物流规划之中吧，这样码头就会有新的运单了。
t_en: Short Auntie just agreed to collaborate with the docks. {NICKNAME}, please add Delicacy Pavilion into the logistics plan. This way, the docks can get some new orders soon.

t_ch: 辛苦你了{F#小囡}{M#小伙子}。我听后厨说你心细得嘞，一个单都没搞错，阿姨太佩服你了。这点辛苦费你一定要收下。
t_en: That was some hard work, {F#young lady}{M#young man}. The kitchen staff told me you didn't get a single dish wrong. Now that's impressive. Here, for your trouble.

t_ch: 辛苦你了{F#小囡}{M#小伙子}。今天的客人很多，一个人在外流浪谋生，肯定很不容易。这点辛苦费你一定要收下。
t_en: That was some hard work, {F#young lady}{M#young man}. It was a busy day today. Poor thing, life must be hard for a wanderer like you. But worry not, I take care of my employee. Here, take this.

t_ch: …其实之前公司也来找过我，希望能把我这店盘下来，我问他们之后作何打算，他们只说不会用来开餐厅，我就告诉他们让我再考虑一下。
t_en: Actually... The IPC came looking for me once before, hoping to buy this place. I asked them what they planned to do with it, and they just said they wouldn't stick a diner here — so I told them that I'd have to think on it.

t_ch: 美馔阁名声没有尚滋味响，要是就这样被收购了，什么都不会留下来，实在太可惜了。所以我想，能不能像燕翠师傅那样做出新菜、赚些名声。
t_en: Delicacy Pavilion might not be as famous as Spices Supreme, but if I just sold out like that, then nothing would be left at all. And wouldn't that be a shame? So I thought, maybe I could make some new dishes like Master Yancui, and earn a little fame and fortune for myself.

t_ch: 但是后来我想通了，我还是想做我擅长的菜，我还想把我这小店开下去…所以，{NICKNAME}，小秘书，我就靠你们帮我把名声打响了。
t_en: But then later on, I figured out that I wanted to cook what I was good at, and I wanted to keep my little diner going... So, {NICKNAME}, and Miss Secretary: I hope you can help me make a name for myself.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，矮阿姨放心吧，希望我们接下来也能合作愉快，一定能让美馔阁声名远扬。
t_en: Hmm, don't worry Short Auntie! I hope we can cooperate again in the future. We'll definitely make a name for Delicacy Pavilion.

t_ch: 在你的帮助下，光顾美馔阁的人越来越多。
t_en: Thanks to your aid, more and more people are visiting Delicacy Pavilion.

t_ch: （罗盘归位的话应该就会招来豺狼吧……）
t_en: (When the compass resets, it will attract those jackals...)

t_ch: 青雀大老爷，您不能总是来找我开病假证明啊，我这是药店，一般不看病。
t_en: Miss Qingque, you can't always come to me for a medical certificate. This is a pharmacy. We don't do diagnosis.

t_ch: 小天老板，你之前可是给我开过病假证明的，可千万不能中途放弃呀！
t_en: Little Tian, you always gave me a medical certificate before. You can't just turn off the tap now!

t_ch: ——不行，我已经得了不治之症了，小天老板你必须救救我，您是丹鼎司出来的对吧？
t_en: No, I've contracted an incurable disease. Little Tian, you have to save me. You're from the Alchemy Commission, right?

t_ch: 青雀大老爷，您还知道丹鼎司啊，您要是有不治之症，就去丹鼎司看病吧！
t_en: Miss Qingque, you know how the Alchemy Commission is. If you've got an incurable disease, go and see them there!

t_ch: 丹鼎司诊断不出我的病，小天老板，你帮我挑个不会露馅的毛病呗？
t_en: The Alchemy Commission can't diagnose my disease, Little Tian. Can you help me pick out a little ailment that won't spill the beans for me?

t_ch: 你就说你这是懒病，绝不会露馅。
t_en: Just say that you're lazy, and you'll never have to reveal anything.

t_ch: 要是懒病能填病假证明，我也想填，但是你懂的。
t_en: If I could use laziness as an excuse on a medical certificate, I would — but you know I can't.

t_ch: 我的客人来了，你先等等。
t_en: I've got a customer. Hold on a second.

t_ch: 这不是{NICKNAME}嘛，我认识的，是我的{F#好姐妹}{M#好兄弟}。
t_en: Well, if it isn't {NICKNAME}, my {F#good sister}{M#good brother}.

t_ch: （好像看到了小天老板的求助眼神，她需要我的帮助……）
t_en: (Little Tian seems to be asking for help with her eyes...)

t_ch: 你先找小天老板吧，我不着急，在这里等等。
t_en: Go ahead and see Little Tian first. I'm in no rush. I'll just wait here.

t_ch: 你可来了！我听说你们商会希望商户们能够加入物流系统之中…我其实挺想加入的，不过我现在想加一个条件，你能不能帮我把这位青雀小姐请走呢？
t_en: You're here! I heard that your guild is trying to port merchants onto the logistics platform... I was actually thinking of signing up, but only on one condition: Can you please help me handle this Qingque lady?

t_ch: 我大概知道是个什么事了。
t_en: I think I know what's going on here.

t_ch: 那可太好了，属实是买金的遇上卖金的。
t_en: That's great. Then it looks like we have a deal.

t_ch: 这是两码事，不能混为一谈。
t_en: These are two separate things. Don't mix them up.

t_ch: 现在「与码头建立合作」和「让青雀小姐放弃开病假证明」已经是一件事了。
t_en: "Making a deal with the docks" and "getting Miss Qingque to give up her quest for a medical certificate" are now the same thing.

t_ch: 求你了，我这也是病急乱投医。
t_en: Please, I'm desperate here.

t_ch: 你放心，我不是甩手掌柜，我知道你反驳她需要证据，你看看我这里有什么能用上的。
t_en: Don't worry. I'm not some lazy taskmaster. I know you need some evidence to refute her. Let's see what I have here.

t_ch: 寿考堂里除了些奇怪的药材之外，你找到了几份可以用来唬人的证据。
t_en: In the Longevity Hall, in addition to the strange medicinal ingredients, you find a few pieces of evidence that are quite convincing.

t_ch: 让我看看，你都找到了些什么…要是这些证据有用的话，我也会从旁协助你的。
t_en: What did you find? Let me have a look.... If these pieces of evidence can help, I'll try to assist you from the side.

t_ch: 《银河通用疾病速查》？
t_en: Pan-Cosmic Quick Diagnosis Booklet?

t_ch: 啊…这个有些陈旧了，这是我为了自己方便而整理的笔记。但是随着化外民大量涌入，各种我没见过的病症也在不断增加。
t_en: Hmm... This one's rather old. Some notes I put together for myself. But with the recent massive influx of Outworlders, I'm seeing outbreaks of all kinds of things that I haven't seen before.

t_ch: 就算是仙舟有着丹鼎司的制药技术，但治病时还得讲究对症下药…所以，化外民也不是想开药就能开药的，需要丹鼎司的药方。
t_en: Even with the pharmaceutical tech of the Alchemy Commission, you still have to find the right cure when treating people... So Outworlders can't just get medical prescriptions just because they want something. They need that prescription from the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: 《琼玉牌秘技丛集》？
t_en: Celestial Jade Techniques 101?

t_ch: …我和青雀小姐是牌友。
t_en: ...Miss Qingque and I are game mates.

t_ch: 但这不是我给她开病假证明的理由。
t_en: But that's not why I gave her a medical certificate.

t_ch: 上一次的病假证明备份？
t_en: A copy of medical certificate from last time?

t_ch: 青雀小姐之前来开过证明，病症是「恹恹病」。不知道银河里是否有这种疑难杂症，「恹恹病」确实是我翻诗集编出来的病状。
t_en: Miss Qingque came to get a medical certificate from me before for "Feebleitis." I'm actually not sure if it's a real thing. I just made up the name by looking through a few poetry books.

t_ch: 好家伙，报应隔天就到。丹鼎司怀疑我的从业水平，让我再次参加丹士资格考试能力检测。哎，不会再有下次了，随便编一个病症是行不通的。
t_en: But no good deed goes unpunished. The Alchemy Commission suspected I faked my medical qualification and told me to resit my alchemist exams. Well, it won't happen again. Making up a random name won't work.

t_ch: 星际和平公司合作邀请函？
t_en: Collaboration Invitation from the IPC?

t_ch: …啊，这个和青雀小姐这件事无关。你知道的，公司几乎向金人巷所有的商户都发送了邀请函，希望商户们能与公司建立合作。
t_en: ...Uh, that's all completely unrelated to Miss Qingque, though. As you know, IPC has sent invites to all guild merchants in Aurum Alley wishing to strike deals with all of them.

t_ch: 大意是希望我转让店铺所有权，获取高额转让费…公司不明白，长生种无所事事比没钱更加可怕，所以商户们也没有立刻倒戈。
t_en: The general idea is for me to transfer ownership rights of the store on to someone, and claim a nice fat transfer fee... But the IPC doesn't understand that having nothing to do is far worse than being poor for a long-life species, so the merchants haven't immediately flipped sides.

t_ch: 那就拜托你了。
t_en: And so I leave it in your hands.

t_ch: 我明白了，交给我吧。
t_en: I see. Leave it to me.

t_ch: 我见过您在码头与人激辩的本事，这次情况虽然不如码头那般针锋相对，但也并不容易。你放心，这是我自己的事，我也积极配合你的。
t_en: I've seen your debating skills at the dock. The situation this time may not be as confrontational as back then, but it's no walk in the park either. Don't worry, this is my own issue, and I'll cooperate with you the best I can.

t_ch: 哎，我知道你是来劝我放弃幻想、认真上班的。但今时不同往日，我有必须翘班的理由。
t_en: Hey, I know you're here to persuade me to give up my delusion so I can get back to work. But this time is different. I really need an excuse to ditch work.

t_ch: 因为负责管理排期的卜官失算，原本应按时完成的占卜事项全都延误了。而那位卜者自知太卜大人生气后果很严重，大笔一挥，就把这些工作量试图转嫁到我们头上……
t_en: The diviner in charge of scheduling made a mistake, and all the divinations ended up getting delayed. And knowing how angry the master diviner would be, with a swipe of the pen, that diviner tried to frame us with that workload...

t_ch: 原本一个月的工作，按照现在的规划，必须在半个月内完成。每一位太卜司卜者都成了这次「延误事件」的受害者……
t_en: According to the current plan, a month's work now has to be done in half a month. Every diviner at the Divination Commission has fallen victim to this "delayed workload"...

t_ch: 我认为这不是你的问题。
t_en: I don't think this is your problem.

t_ch: 我也认为自己不应该为他人的失误买单。
t_en: I agree that I shouldn't have to pay for other people's mistakes.

t_ch: 但是呢，那位卜官话说的可漂亮了，「咱们可是一个集体呀！加把劲，努努力，办法总比困难多！」
t_en: But, that diviner put it nicely — "We are a collective! Knuckle down, work hard, and there's nothing we can't get done!"

t_ch: 反正她只是个监工罢了，又不会下场干活。
t_en: Anyway, she's just a supervisor and would never roll her sleeves up and get her hands dirty.

t_ch: 你应该反抗，直接罢工！
t_en: You should go on strike! Stand up for yourselves!

t_ch: 那样的话，我就连薪水都拿不到了。
t_en: If we do that, I won't even be able to draw a salary.

t_ch: 我要是有那胆子，我费劲请病假是要做什么啊。
t_en: If I had the courage for that, what would I need to get a healer's note?

t_ch: 你要在这种时候请病假吗？
t_en: Are you really going to ask for sick leave at a time like this?

t_ch: 关键时期，太卜司缺了谁都不行——但是缺了「生病的青雀」，行！
t_en: The Divination Commission needs everyone in a crisis like this — except a sick Qingque!

t_ch: 但是病假证明不是万能的。
t_en: But a medical certificate can't beat everything.

t_ch: 我明白你什么意思，你认为我是在做无用功。
t_en: I get what you mean. You think my job is useless.

t_ch: 这样吧，我拿着「病假证明」，你扮演「审核病假的卜者」，现在就来对质一番。
t_en: How about this: I'll take a medical certificate, and you play the role of "sick leave auditor-diviner." C'mon, let's play it out.

t_ch: 如果我的理由完美无缺，你就不要继续拦着我了；如果我的理由站不住脚……
t_en: If my excuse is watertight, then you've no right to stop me. But if my reason is deemed groundless...

t_ch: 那你就打消请假的念头吧。
t_en: Then you can give up on the thought of taking leave.

t_ch: 再去找个更好的理由！
t_en: Come up with a better excuse!

t_ch: 一言为定。
t_en: Deal.

t_ch: 您好，我是太卜司的司库青雀，我很抱歉地通知您，由于前些天我感染了「不治之症」，身体状况不佳，需要请一段时间病假进行修养和康复。
t_en: Hello there, I'm the custodian, Qingque, from the Divination Commission. I sincerely regret to inform you that I am feeling unwell due to contracting an "incurable disease," and must request a period of sick leave in which to rest and recuperate.

t_ch: 我出现了大量脱发、浑身头疼、肝部肿胀等症状，严重影响了我的日常工作。为了避免病情恶化，传染其他卜者，我应该积极采取治疗措施。
t_en: My symptoms include hair loss, headache in my arm, and a swelling liver, all of which severely impact my daily work duties. To avoid my condition worsening or infecting any other diviners, I should take active treatment measures.

t_ch: 我请求在接下来<unbreak>10</unbreak>天内请假，以便我能够全面恢复并回到工作中。我保证尽快恢复健康，并在回到工作时，尽力补偿请假期间的工作进度。
t_en: I am requesting a leave of absence for the next ten days so that I may fully recuperate and return to work duties. I pledge to recover as soon as possible and will do my best to make up for lost progress once I return to work.

t_ch: 感谢您的理解和支持，期待您的批准！
t_en: Thank you for your support and looking forward to your approval!

t_ch: 这说法实在是太标准了。
t_en: This really is quite the bureaucratese.

t_ch: 熟练运用请假语是打工人的基础技能哦。
t_en: Proficiency surrounding the language used when requesting leave is a basic skill for salary workers.

t_ch: 这是你平时说话的语气吗？
t_en: Is that how you normally talk?

t_ch: …怎么可能，这是请假专用语。
t_en: ...What, no! This is how I talk when I want to request sick leave.

t_ch: 等等，我记得你在码头上怼过公司代表，你不会也想对我也来上这么一套吧？
t_en: Wait, I remember you turning that IPC representative inside out on the docks with your words. You're not going to do that to me, are you?

t_ch: 激辩开始！
t_en: The debate starts now!

t_ch: 等等！等等！我只是想请个假而已，用不着这么复杂。
t_en: Wait! Hold on! I just want to take some time off! There's no need to make this so complicated.

t_ch: 我会尽量减少攻击性。
t_en: I'll try to tone down my aggression.

t_ch: 那我们就放轻松，把这个请假流程简化一下。
t_en: We should relax and just keep this whole leave application process straight and simple.

t_ch: 那我们简化一下。
t_en: Then, let's simplify things.

t_ch: 好好好，不要那么严肃，我的理由要真是站不住脚，你就让着我点嘛……
t_en: Fine, fine, fine. No need to be so serious. If my excuses are really that shaky, just go easy on me a bit...

t_ch: 我们先从你第一句开始吧。
t_en: I'll let you start it off, then.

t_ch: 您好，我是太卜司的司库青雀，我很抱歉地通知您，由于前些天我感染了「不治之症」，身体状况不佳，需要请一段时间病假进行修养和康复。
t_en: Hello there, I'm the custodian, Qingque, from the Divination Commission. I sincerely regret to inform you that I am feeling unwell due to contracting an "incurable disease," and must request a period of sick leave in which to rest and recuperate.

t_ch: 「不治之症」是个什么症？
t_en: What is this "incurable disease"?

t_ch: 没错，你这都是「不治之症」了，我怎么治得好？
t_en: Right, you said this disease is "incurable." How am I supposed to treat that?

t_ch: 我这不是还在等小天老板的病假证明吗？那我现在随便取个名字好了——就叫它「不打牌我就要死了综合症」吧。
t_en: Aren't I still waiting to get a medical certificate from Little Tian? Let me come up with a random name for my illness — what about "No-Game-No-Life Syndrome"?

t_ch: 您好，我是太卜司的司库青雀，我很抱歉地通知您，由于前些天我感染了「不打牌我就要死了综合症」，身体状况不佳，需要请一段时间病假进行修养和康复。
t_en: Hello there, I'm the custodian, Qingque, from the Divination Commission. I sincerely regret to inform you that I am feeling unwell due to contracting the No-Game-No-Life Syndrome, and must request a period of sick leave during which to rest and recuperate.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，怎么办，我需要反驳青雀小姐的证据……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, what do I do? I need to refute Miss Qingque's evidence...

t_ch: 《银河通用疾病速查》？
t_en: Pan-Cosmic Quick Diagnosis Booklet?

t_ch: 这是我为了自己方便而整理的笔记，上面记录着大量闻所未闻的奇难怪病。
t_en: These are the notes I listed for my own convenience. I recorded a lot of strange and unheard-of ailments within them.

t_ch: 可是这本《速查》上并没有你的这个「不打牌我就要死了综合症」！
t_en: But this quick diagnosis booklet doesn't have your "No-Game-No-Life Syndrome" listed!

t_ch: 你都说了这是你自己整理的笔记嘛，你把我这个「不打牌我就要死了综合症」加上去不就解决了吗？
t_en: Didn't you say these are your personal notes? Won't the problem be solved if you just add in my "No-Game-No-Life Syndrome"?

t_ch: 我、我……
我竟然哑口无言…{NICKNAME}，我们还有其他的证据吗？
t_en: I'm actually speechless... {NICKNAME}, do we have any other evidence or anything?

t_ch: 《琼玉牌秘技丛集》？
t_en: Celestial Jade Techniques 101?

t_ch: …这是青雀小姐送给我的《琼玉牌秘技丛集》，这是青雀贿赂我的证据。
t_en: ...This is the Celestial Jade Techniques 101 that Miss Qingque gave me! It's evidence of her bribery.

t_ch: 我是你的牌友，又收了你的好处，我才不得不给你开出病假证明的！
t_en: It's because I'm your game mate and have also "benefited" from our friendship that I had to give you this sick leave note!

t_ch: 你说的没错，但是我请假的时候，绝不会告诉别人的！这样是不会暴露什么的。
t_en: Exactly! But when I take my absence, I won't tell anyone! That way, no one will notice a thing.

t_ch: 我、我……
…{NICKNAME}，青雀说的有道理，这本《琼玉牌秘技丛集》好像用处不大。
t_en: ...{NICKNAME}, Qingque has a point. The Celestial Jade Techniques 101 isn't actually that useful.

t_ch: 上一次的病假证明备份？
t_en: A copy of medical certificate from last time?

t_ch: 这是上一次用过的病假证明……
t_en: This is the medical certificate that was used last time...

t_ch: 对对对，就是这个，你直接把病名改成「不打牌我就要死了综合症」不就成了吗？毕竟银河里有着各式各样的病症……
t_en: Yeah, yeah! This one! Just change my ailment to "No-Game-No-Life Syndrome" and everything will be fine? I mean, there are all sorts of weird conditions across the cosmos...

t_ch: 小天老板<color=#dbc291ff>随便编一个病症</color>，只要看上去合情合理就没人会查的，上次就是这样。
t_en: Little Tian, you just need to <color=#dbc291ff>randomly make a disease up</color>. Nobody's going to check up on that — provided it looks reasonable. That's how it went down last time.

t_ch: 临时编个病症是行不通的，丹鼎司专门派人找过我，确认病因病状是否有医典记录。
t_en: Making up an illness on the spot won't get you anywhere. The Alchemy Commission has sent someone after me before to check if the symptoms I listed are recorded in their medical texts.

t_ch: 什么？！丹鼎司竟然还有这么敬业的人！
t_en: What!? Don't tell me the Alchemy Commission turned out to be this thorough with all their work?

t_ch: 如果是这样的话，我倒是不想连累小天老板…我再想想别的法子吧。
t_en: If that's so, then I don't want to get Little Tian in trouble... I'll have to come up with something else.

t_ch: 星际和平公司合作邀请函？
t_en: Collaboration Invitation from the IPC?

t_ch: 公司几乎向金人巷所有的商户都发送了邀请函，希望商户们能与公司建立合作，我还有些犹豫……
t_en: The IPC had sent invites to almost all the stores in Aurum Alley, wishing to strike deals with us all. I'm still a bit hesitant...

t_ch: 要是公司标下了金人巷，也许以后就没有寿考堂给你开病假了。
t_en: If the IPC manages to secure Aurum Alley, there may not be a Longevity Hall here anymore to give you sick leave notes.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}不会让公司得逞的。
t_en: {NICKNAME} won't let them succeed.

t_ch: 看来这个证据也不合适，我们换个别的证据吧。
t_en: Looks like this piece of evidence doesn't work either. Let's try something else.

t_ch: 没想到在这里也能听到你的「激辩」…就结果而言，您应该是成功了吧？
t_en: I didn't expect to hear you "debating" here... As far as the verdict was concerned, you won, right?
